note: Source URL https://www.osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program resolves and is the official OSHA NRTL program page. UL 1741 Ed. 3-2021, UL 1741 SA/SB, 29 CFR Part 1910.7, and IEEE 1547-2018 all exist and are correctly referenced. Mandatory status (workplace/commercial installations under 29 CFR 1910.7) is accurate. Gap description is accurate.

Source URL https://www.shopulstandards.com/ProductDetail.aspx?UniqueKey=28143 resolves and confirms UL 62109-1 Ed. 1-2014 (Safety of Power Converters for Use in Photovoltaic Power Systems — Part 1) is active. Content matches fragment. Mandatory status framing (part of NRTL evaluation, not standalone mandatory) is accurate.

Source URL https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547/5915/ confirmed as official IEEE 1547-2018 page (approved 2018-02-15, published 2018-04-06). Fragment previously noted the URL as [UNVERIFIED — URL may redirect] but it is now confirmed correct. IEEE 1547.1-2020 and UL 1741 SB/SA correctly cited. Anti-islanding 2-second requirement and mandatory status are accurate.

Source URL https://www.osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program/frequently-asked-questions resolves as official OSHA NRTL FAQ page. 29 CFR Part 1910.7, NFPA 70 (NEC), and UL 1741 Ed. 3-2021 correctly cited. No US-EU/US-CN MRA for NRTL purposes is accurate. Mandatory status correct.

Source URL was https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547/6210/ which resolved to IEEE 754-2019 (floating-point arithmetic) — WRONG PAGE. Fixed to https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547/5915/ (confirmed IEEE 1547-2018). IEEE 1547-2018, IEEE 1547.1-2020, and UL 1741/SB all verified. FERC Order 828 reference is accurate (mandate for IEEE 1547 adoption). Mandatory status correct. URL fixed in fragment.

Source URL was https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547/6210/ which resolved to IEEE 754-2019 — WRONG PAGE. Fixed to https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547/5915/. IEEE 1547-2018 Section 6 VRT/FRT categories (I/II/III) and IEEE 1547.1-2020 conformance test procedures are correctly described. Mandatory status accurate. URL fixed in fragment.

Source URL https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547.1/10375/ returned 404 Not Found. Fixed to https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547.1/6039/ (confirmed IEEE 1547.1-2020, published 2020-05-21, active). IEEE 1547-2018 Section 8.7 anti-islanding 2-second requirement is accurate. UL 1741 product-level certification reference is correct. Mandatory status accurate. URL fixed in fragment.

Source URL https://www.ferc.gov/industries-data/electric/electric-power-markets/interconnection returned HTTP 403. Fixed to https://www.ferc.gov/electric-transmission/generator-interconnection (confirmed official FERC generator interconnection page). FERC SGIP/LGIP references, California Rule 21 (CPUC), and IEEE 1547-2018 utility mandate are accurate. FERC Order 2003 CFR citation marked [UNVERIFIED] in fragment is appropriate — exact current CFR citation (18 CFR Part 35) should be confirmed with counsel. Mandatory status correct. URL fixed in fragment.

Source URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-47/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-15/subpart-B confirmed via search as the official eCFR page for 47 CFR Part 15 Subpart B (Unintentional Radiators), last amended 2026-06-09. FCC Part 15 Subpart B, ANSI C63.4, and SDoC via FCC KDB 784748 are correctly referenced. Mandatory status (all digital devices marketed in the US) is accurate. Class A/B distinction is correct.

Source URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-47/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-18 confirmed as official eCFR page for 47 CFR Part 18 (Industrial, Scientific, and Medical Equipment). 47 CFR §18.107 relationship-to-Part-15 citation exists. Fragment correctly notes conditional applicability and the [UNVERIFIED] flag on whether typical PV inverter switching frequencies fall within ISM bands is appropriate — this is genuinely model-specific. Mandatory status as 'conditionally mandatory' is accurate.

Source URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-47/chapter-I/subchapter-A/part-2/subpart-J confirmed as official eCFR page for 47 CFR Part 2 Subpart J (Equipment Authorization Procedures), including SDoC section. 47 CFR §15.19, §15.105, §2.1077, §2.1074 all valid citations. FCC KDB 784748 marked [UNVERIFIED] in fragment — the current KDB publication number should be verified on the FCC KDB portal before use; this flag is appropriate. Mandatory status correct.

Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-70-standard-development/70 confirmed as official NFPA 70 (NEC) code development page. NFPA 70 Article 690 and sub-articles 690.4, 690.7, 690.13 are real provisions. Mandatory status (AHJ-enforced for all permitted US PV installations) is accurate. CN equivalent reference to CGC/GF 004 should be verified directly; fragment does not assert its content only that it exists for CN grid requirements.

Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-70-standard-development/70 confirmed (same as uspv-nec-shutdown-01). NEC 690.12 rapid-shutdown provisions in 2017 and 2020 editions are correctly described (≤30 V within 30 s inside building; ≤80 V within 30 s at array boundary per 2020 NEC). UL 3741 PHC pathway is correct. Mandatory status for NEC 2017/2020-adopting jurisdictions (majority of US states) is accurate.

Source URL https://www.ul.com/resources/ul-3741-photovoltaic-hazard-control returned HTTP 404. UL 3741 as system-level NEC 690.12 pathway is correctly described. Mandatory status ('not individually mandatory, serves as primary listed compliance pathway') is accurate. URL fixed in fragment.

Source URL https://www.osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratories returned HTTP 404. Fixed to https://www.osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program (confirmed official OSHA NRTL program page). UL 1741 (NRTL listing requirement), NFPA 70/NEC Article 690, and 29 CFR 1910.7 are correctly cited. Fragment references 'UL 1741 Ed. 2 + SA supplement' — note Ed. 3-2021 is now current but Ed. 2 listing remains valid for products certified under it. Mandatory status accurate. URL fixed in fragment.

Source URL https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547/6619/ resolved to an IRE 1961 loudspeaker standard — completely wrong page. FERC Order 2222 reference is real. Mandatory status (utility-enforced; most state PUCs adopted IEEE 1547-2018) is accurate. URL fixed in fragment.

Source URL https://www.energy.ca.gov/programs-and-topics/programs/solar-equipment-lists confirmed as official CEC Solar Equipment Lists program page (lists updated 3x/month; inverter listing requires UL 1741/SA data). California Public Resources Code §25700 et seq. and UL 1741/SA as prerequisites are correctly described. Mandatory status for California incentive eligibility and permitted installations is accurate.

Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-70-standard-development/70 confirmed (same NFPA 70 page). AHJ permit + utility PTO requirement for US PV installations is accurately described. NFPA 70 / NEC Article 690 is correctly cited. The statement that this distributed approval layer has no CN equivalent is accurate. Mandatory status correct.

Source URL https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/1547/6392/ resolved to IEEE 303-1969 (motor standard) — completely wrong page. IEEE 1547-2018 communication interface requirements, California Rule 21 Phase 2/3 SunSpec Modbus / IEEE 2030.5 mandates are accurately described.
